p1-review-ux-blind-approval-and-checklis.notes: You're cofounding two thing the review of the code itself, and that is not something that we have scoped in debussy so far. I'm talking about the review of the artifacts. Let's take the research plan implement workflow which produce artifact at each step. It's very powerfull, but if there is too much friction in the reviewing process, it's loosing most of its edge.

p2-long-workflow-monitoring-opacity-in-m.notes: it's mostly about observability of long workflow, not so much about failures.

p3-parallel-lanes-worktree-orchestration.notes: yes it does work, but what i find painful is to understant which session is in which worktree and i lack a process to stage one worktree. Let's say we have 3 worktrees, worktree A, B and C, once worktree C is ready for review, i want it to be "staged" (not like local change) i mean brough to the root folder as a re gular branch. I do the review and then push it back to the background, to worktree, and call worktree B to be staged, tested, etc...

p4-context-amnesia-after-compaction.notes: this is weirdly angled. the goal is to have a hioghly detailed, granular and researchable documentation that describe everything in the code base, from feature, to architecture decision, to purpose, to a testing stratgey, etc... have a very standardised organisation of that documentation avoid the inflation of the document base and facilitate it's usage.

p5-plugin-context-budget-pressure.notes: Not quite right. It's about building observability in the claude setup. plugin, skills, commands, agents, etc...

p6-ai-output-quality-requires-separate-r.notes: that's a valid issue, but not in the scope of Debussy...

p7-cost-anxiety-and-token-waste.notes: Not really in the scope of Debussy. Might be a side quest to monitor where the cost go (by feature, skills and everything) but it's jsut about monitoring.
